meaning: n.嫁接,(接技用的)嫩枝,(皮肤等的)移植,赎职v.嫁接,接技,移植(皮肤等),赎职
examples: The skin graft failed to take.
To prep the eye to receive the tooth and lens, the doctors placed a graft over the eye to promote moisture.
I am having a skin graft on my arm soon.
There's also a 5% chance that the graft will slip, requiring an additional operation.
Spaniards have long suspected that there is graft in their town halls, whichever party runs them.
